channel.title: Posts on Aaditya Maheshwari
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Aaditya Maheshwari

item.title: Just Words
item.description: Some words, some lines. Up to you what you make of them. Energy follows thought. Actions express priorities. He is able who thinks he is able. All humans die. But few have lived. Life is either a daring adventure or nothing. It’s not what you are underneath, it’s what you do that defines you. Pain & ecstasy come hand in hand. How will you use your gifts? What choices will you make?

item.title: AngularJS - Live search
item.description: I love to see web pages & apps with the live search feature. In fact, they are perfect for SPAs (Single Page Applications). My MovieFlix app written in MEAN has something similar. I am gonna share something very close to that here. The only difference you’ll notice is the use of scope in angular & where it applies i.e. change & filter. Also, this is not a tutorial post, so don’t expect any explanations here.

item.title: Games I play regularly
item.description: Following are some games (PC) that I play often & never get bored: Counter Strike: Global Offensive (CS:GO) -> First-person shooter (FPS) genre FIFA -> Sport genre Fortnite Battle Royale -> Battle Royale genre Overwatch -> FPS genre I never get bored playing these games. They don’t have a fixed storyline or a campaign that repeats over & over again. All these games have great support from the companies & the community.

item.title: What's on my android
item.description: I am a simple man & that is how I like my phone: simple! Stock android, fast, smooth, no bloatware & nothing fancy. Just usability, good UX, intelligent AI, assistant & productivity but with the ability to customize whatever whenever need be. Hence, I own a Google Pixel 2 XL. Not only Google phones run stock android but I also like the front facing stereo speakers. Not to mention the camera on the Pixel 2 XL is the best even when it is not a dual camera.

item.title: A simple web server in Go (Golang)
item.description: This is a simple Go web server I came across on a Github Gist & I liked it. So I am sharing it with everyone. I plan on using this with some modifications in my projects for its simplicity. It comes with basic logging, tracing, health check, graceful shutdown and zero dependencies. package main import ( "context" "flag" "fmt" "log" "net/http" "os" "os/signal" "sync/atomic" "time" ) type key int const ( requestIDKey key = 0 ) var ( listenAddr string healthy int32 ) func main() { flag.

item.title: My favorite VS Code extensions
item.description: VS Code is great code editor which has some awesome basic features without the need of extensions/plugins than most of the other code editors. Yet there are some extensions that I like using with VS Code to accelerate my productivity, make the editor look even more awesome & linting etc. Better HAML - For Ruby on Rails projects that use HAML. I prefer HAML over html.erb because it is much cleaner & easier to read.

item.title: Visual Studio Code - My favorite code editor
item.description: Everything below about VS Code are my opinions & all about personal preference. Yes! Visual Studio Code (VS Code) is my favorite & to go code editor. I have used Sublime Text & Atom too. I was great fan of Atom until I used VS Code. I have been using VS Code for almost a year now. I recently did give Atom (Version 1.23.3) a try again but there was still a huge performance difference when compared to VS Code.

item.title: Should you learn TypeScript?
item.description: What is TypeScript anyway? Should you know/learn TypeScript? TypeScript (TS) is a typed super-set of JavaScript (JS) which compiles to plain JavaScript. TypeScripts adds some new ECMAScript features too. The type system of TS is relatively rich and includes: interfaces, enums, hybrid types, generics, union & intersection types, access modifiers and much more. Check the official TS page out to learn more about TypeScript. All JS code is valid TS code.

item.title: JSON cheat sheet
item.description: JSON (Javascript Object notation) is a syntax & a standard for storing & exchanging data, specifically between the server & the client (like a browser or the front-end). JSON is “self-defined” or say “self-described”. It is easy to understand & learn. The JSON format was first specified by Douglas Crockford. It was extended from the Javascript scripting language. JSON is language independent. Every JSON file has the extension .json & the internet media type/Multipurpose Internet Mail Extensions (MIME) type is application/json while the uniform type identifier is public.

item.title: Markdown Cheat Sheet
item.description: # Table of Contents 1. [Headers](#headers)2. [Text Styling](#text-styling)3. [Lists](#lists)4. [Links](#links)5. [Images](#images)6. [Code & Syntax Highlighting](#code-and-syntax-highlighting)7. [Tables](#tables)8. [Blockquotes](#blockquotes)9. [Inline HTML](#inline-hTML)10. [Horizontal Rule](#horizontal-rule)11. [Line Breaks](#line-breaks)12. [YouTube Videos](#youtube-videos) Table of Contents Headers Text Styling Lists Links Images Code & Syntax Highlighting Tables Blockquotes Inline HTML Horizontal Rule Line Breaks YouTube Videos # Headers # H1 ## H2 ### H3 #### H4 ##### H5 ###### H6 Headers H1 H2 H3 H4 H5 H6 # Text Styling *This text will be italic* _This will also be italic_ **This text will be bold**** __This will also be bold____ _You **can** combine them_Combined emphasis with **asterisks and _underscores_****.
